measure.note: MEASURE (post-run, out-of-band, NON-BLOCKING -- reused for triage, parallel to the run's own gates). REUSE: the node's own checks.post only asserts audio-gate.json is non-empty + valid JSON -- it never asserts the gate's own verdict. HARDENED (adversarial pass, 2026-07-09): was a whole-file `regex-present` scan for `"pass":\s*true`, which matches ANY occurrence of that substring anywhere in the file, not specifically the TOP-LEVEL field -- a nested per-finding "pass" key (or a hand-edited artifact) would false-PASS regardless of the real verdict. Now JSON.parse()s the report and reads `.pass` at the top level explicitly via scripts/measure.mjs (no new SDK code -- the same run+writes op shape onset-augmentation-coverage already used). Exit 0 = top-level pass===true; exit 1 with an explicit `error` string = pass is false/missing OR the file is missing/unreadable/unparseable (fail-closed, never a silent skip).

MEASURE (post-run, out-of-band, NON-BLOCKING) -- asserts the design promise the prompt states verbatim (STEP1: 'truncation NEVER sets pass:false'): truncationFails stays 0 even though truncationAdvisories may be non-zero. HARDENED (adversarial pass, 2026-07-09): same whole-file-substring fragility as audio-gate-pass-floor above -- now JSON.parse()s and reads the top-level `truncationFails` field explicitly via scripts/measure.mjs. Exit 0 = top-level truncationFails===0; exit 1 = nonzero/missing/unreadable/unparseable (fail-closed).

MEASURE -- the COUNT half stays a GRADED count, deliberately NOT a gate on raw coverage (Part D thin wrapper; not every cue needs onsets -- criteria.md R4, which CAN read the storyboard's teaching actions, judges that). HARDENED (adversarial pass, 2026-07-09), three proven holes closed via scripts/measure.mjs: (1) the prior inline one-liner counted a cue as 'has onsets' if the STRING 'tokenOnsets' appeared on the SAME LINE as its `id:` -- keys on line-adjacency, breaks under pretty-printing, and a `tokenOnsets: []` (empty, present, vacuous) satisfied it. Now object-scopes the parse (a string-aware brace scanner extracts each cue's `{ ... }` literal regardless of line breaks) and VALIDATES: tokenOnsets must be non-empty, length-matched to targetTokens, monotonic (non-decreasing), and every value in-window [0, narrationFrames) -- a present-but-invalid tokenOnsets is now a MALFORMED-onsets HARD FAIL (exit 1), never silently counted as coverage. (2) an unreadable/missing asr-alignment.json or Clips.ts now reports an explicit `asrError`/`error` string and exits 1, distinct from a genuinely-empty-but-valid `{totalCues:0}` (no error field, exit 0) -- previously both degraded to the identical-looking `{totalCues:0, cuesWithOnsets:0}`, a false green. (3) lessonId/paths are discovered from this run's OWN recorded `.pi/run.json` artifacts, never from a run-argument placeholder token (robust across runs that predate arg-persistence -- see w2b-audio-captions/scripts/measure.mjs precedent). VALIDATED against the real artifact: kptest-count-to-two now reports {totalCues:3, cuesWithOnsets:0, malformedOnsets:[]} -- correctly counts the documented gap (memory.md) with zero false MALFORMED flags on a legitimately onset-absent lesson.
